rationale: Qwen3.5-397B linear-attention (GatedDeltaNet) heads have widely different decay/memory. Short-memory heads use a TOKEN window: window-outside history is reused from the decayed prefix status, window-inside is recomputed (lossless when window >= head memory). GLOBAL heads relay full status; LOCAL heads reset to decayed prefix at window boundaries.

moe.note: Deep MoE layers (>= deep_moe_start_layer=24, i.e. the deep 36 of 60 layers) let tokens with attention-mass < mass_thresh*mean SKIP routed experts (keep shared only). Mass is collected from the deep half of full-attention layers. Sweet spot from the 397B sweep: deep_moe_start=24 + mass_thresh=0.7 maximized MoE compute saving (~46% total) while staying lossless.

summary.note: 397B sweet spot: shallow layers (<24) keep dense-token MoE; deep layers (>=24) use sparse-token MoE skipping ~53% low-mass tokens. window_tok[head] = clamp(ceil(safety * mem_len_token), min_window); >= context => GLOBAL. Layers < dense_prefix_layers stay dense.
